++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++: Nothing is pretty easy to imagine, in my experience. To imagine, though. To comprehend it is a beast of an undertaking I'd rather leave to you. 23/03/2026
llllllllllllllllllll++lllllwfgllllllllll: I've always despised looking in mirrors. Sucking in the girth to look presentable, holding my breath until the result registers in my brain as my normal. I wish I could look at my body and see a person, deserving of love, without meaningful flaws at all. But that's not who I am. It's not going to work. I've settled for something simpler. I want to change the way I think, not the way I look. I've started to make progress; now, finally, I can look in the mirror, see all these imperfections and blisters and redness, and find it at least interesting. If not beautiful, I can be interesting. 24/03/2026
++++++++++++++x++hhRRhh++hhRRhh+++++++++: Today, I and a few friends gathered and discussed our favorite topics, including yaoi and K-Pop and participated in very uncouth activities. We had a project to cover, for a language class, in which we replicate a music video using American Sign Language. One person involved was remarkably capable of learning the proper order and sign parameters, even though she lacked any education in the language, as a Latin student. Once we prepared ourselves, by watching videos about the group we were to mimic, we drove out to a countryside road. We filmed, we signed, we acted out a car crash, and so forth. It is, of course, necessary for a person to wave to all they pass by. Interestingly, I have found that waving two-handedly with a big smile, perhaps leaning in and crouching slightly, is endearing enough from most people to ease tensions, with someone you can't immediately talk to, for example if someone encounters you along their private road. Then, we went to a gas station, an airport, filming our parts there, before making our way to a pizzeria. There, we filmed briefly, finishing the shoot, before ordering food and eating. We repeated a bit we created in the airport, in which my friend was holding a Monster Energy, and I grabbed him by the throat, saying that he was not allowed to bring drinks into the plane. We giggled about it incessantly. When we got home, my friend brought out a homemade cake, much to our collective delight. We ate, noting that the heap of sprinkles atop the cake tasted of joyous shards of glass, and moved on. Getting to my own home, I played with my bunnies, who I will now introduce as Hali, named after the mausoleum at Halicarnassus, and Giza, which you can assume the origin of. They have recently become far less skittish, allowing direct approach with minimal recoil, and passing by without backing away. I have rewarded them with vitamin cookies. Notably, I took one's away as to tease them, but they didn't notice fast enough to grow mad, only realizing long after it was out of sight and therefore untraceable back to me. They seemed to tear up, though not really, so I gave it back, and the poor thing seemed so enamored by the fact that they had received two cookies that day, though I had only regiven them their original cookie. Also, before I went to my friend's house, I had been messing about with Scratch. I find it very fun to create complex things with such simple tools. A few days before, I had created a tool to map 3D points to a perspective camera, which was delightfully successful. As of now, I have yet to figure out how to use this in any actual games, as I don't know how to cull any faces properly, breaking the whole thing. Today, I tested a markov chain tool, as I intended to design a behavioral loop for NPCs in my games. It has worked wonderfully so far, and now I am setting up evolving these chains. Anyway, I went to bed after I played with my bunnies, saying goodnight to my parents. I write from bed. Goodnight. 15/02/2026
Today, I enjoyed a can of tea with my friend. I hung out at their house after playing rhythm games and eating a bear claw, at which time we shared memes and ate rice. We went on errands, first returning an item before going grocery shopping. Finally, we ate pasta and watched Jumanji, and I got to have another bear claw. I have lost passion for this diary entry. Goodnight. 21/02/2026
